opp.ais_why:
• Product scope is AI-rich with three AI modules (ANA, Merlin, Merlin Intake) alongside iContract, iSource, iSupplier, eInvoicing, iRisk at $1.2M ACV. Merlin flag is true and Merlin_Products lists 'ANA, Merlin, Merlin Intake' ‚Äî three AI SKUs is a substantial product-level AI signal. Flagship IT & Consulting industry account with strategic logo value.
• Customer_Business_Problem captures the rationale verbatim: 'Bosch operates with highly fragmented systems (~40 ERPs) and heterogeneous procurement processes. Current indirect procurement lacks efficiency, automation, data consistency, supplier risk transparency, and user experience. Bosch wants one unified, modern, scalable S2P platform.' Comments_on_Qualification: 'Entire S2P project global'. Shortlisted stage with LastActivityDate 11 May 2026 indicating active late-stage engagement.
• Customer-stated narrative centers on platform consolidation, automation, data consistency and user experience rather than AI-led transformation. Business_Objectives is logged only as 'Done' without AI specifics, and Description is blank. Three AI modules in scope show product-level AI footprint but boundary defaulted DOWN at 6/7 without explicit prospect-side AI champion or selection criterion in allowed evidence. Score 6 reflects rich AI scope + platform-consolidation narrative without AI-as-selection-criterion.

items.area: Economic buyer / budget owner identity and engagement
items.quote: John suggested to engage with c level once we are through the current stage of testing

items.quote: We are in a long-list of 4-5 suppliers.
items.source: Salesforce Next Step (2 Feb 2026); no rival named on any call

items.area: Euro-denominated ROI / business case owned by Bosch finance
items.quote: Business_Objectives logged only as 'Done' without AI or savings specifics

metrics.narrative: Bosch articulated clear scale and automation drivers: highly fragmented systems (~40 ERPs) and heterogeneous indirect-procurement processes lacking efficiency, automation, data consistency, supplier-risk transparency and user experience, with a stated goal of one unified, modern, scalable S2P platform (Customer_Business_Problem__c). The prior CRM MEDDPICC (13 May 2026) quantified the volume envelope: support for roughly +50% user growth and +100% transaction growth. What is still missing is a Bosch-owned, euro-denominated savings or ROI case agreed with their finance lead; the value language remains Zycus's, not the buyer's. Building that quantified business case with Bosch finance stays a ranked move.

champion.narrative: Operational advocacy is broad and genuine but has not reached power level. Thomas Kirmse (Category Purchasing Software & IT Services) is the named champion candidate and commercial point of contact, is 'open for communications', and the Next Step shows a scheduled call with him on 25 Jun, yet he appears on few calls and his internal advocacy is not yet evidenced. Sabina Nordmann coordinated the entire on-site POC as project lead and Rebecca Elias delivered the structured, constructive closing assessment, both giving Zycus deep access and positive feedback. What is still missing is a champion who is actively selling Zycus to the budget owner against the other tender finalists; converting Kirmse (or recruiting an exec sponsor) into that internal seller is the priority.

competition.narrative: Bosch is running a structured 4-5 vendor tender: Salesforce Next Step (2 Feb 2026) records 'we are in a long-list of 4-5 suppliers' with awarding targeted for July, and on 22 May Thomas Kirmse confirmed Bosch will 'close the PoC with all market participants' before deciding, so multiple rivals remain live. No competitor has been named by Bosch on any of the ~28 touchpoints, so the specific rival logos are a genuine knowledge gap; the only explicitly characterised alternative is the status-quo fragmented best-of-breed stack the platform would replace. Zycus's wedge is its organically-built agentic AI (Merlin) and proven live SAP S/4HANA integration via Rojo adapters, both surfaced in the POC; the risk is an unnamed rival building the economic-buyer relationship first, so uncovering the named shortlist from Kirmse is a ranked move.

identify_pain.narrative: The pain is specific and buyer-confirmed: Bosch operates highly fragmented systems (~40 ERPs) and heterogeneous indirect-procurement processes with poor efficiency, automation, data consistency, supplier-risk transparency and user experience, and wants one unified, modern, scalable S2P platform (Customer_Business_Problem__c; 'Entire S2P project global'). During the POC Bosch probed exactly these pains, including complex sourcing (auctions, non-tender allocation), seamless sourcing-to-contracting, and ESG / supplier-risk steering, and Rebecca Elias's 22 May findings confirmed both the fit and the residual out-of-the-box gaps. The pain owners are the operational procurement and IT-process teams (Nordmann, Walterspacher, Streitenberger) who will become the platform's power admins.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are only partly visible. Thomas Kirmse confirmed on 22 May that Bosch will 'first close the PoC with all market participants' and then 'define the next strategy', moving into commercial and contract discussions expected in June; Dirk Fischbach committed to provide the implementation-partner matrix, which was sent to Thomas on 22 May (EY previously ruled out as a partner). A Cloud SaaS security questionnaire and a Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR) RFI were issued during the POC, signalling formal procurement and InfoSec gating ahead. No MSA / SOW, legal review or signature authority has been discussed yet, and a system integrator is expected to join for implementation. What would create visibility is a mutual close plan naming the pricing, security-review, legal and partner steps against dated owners.

sources: Avoma call manifest 19 Feb-26 May 2026 (EB absent)
economic_buyer.narrative: After seven months, a $1.2M deal and a two-week on-site POC, the budget owner has never appeared on any recorded call, which is the single biggest structural risk on this deal. Every engaged stakeholder is operational (procurement and IT-process), and on 12 May the note 'John suggested to engage with c level once we are through the current stage of testing' shows C-level contact was consciously deferred; the POC finished 22 May with no C-level engagement since. What the evidence does show is that Thomas Kirmse (Category Purchasing Software & IT Services) owns the commercial and partner thread and is the route to power. Securing an C-level meeting via Kirmse before the July award is a ranked move.

decision_process.narrative: The process is well mapped from the call and activity sequence: RFI submitted Dec 2025, RFP invitation 12 Jan 2026, Q&A via the sourcing platform Jan 2026, shortlist confirmation 27 Mar 2026, a two-week on-site POC 11-22 May, a POC closing/findings review 22 May, and now RFP round 2 (documents received 29 Jun, response due 17 Jul) running in parallel with implementation-partner selection. On the buyer's stated timeline awarding is targeted for July, project start August 2026 and go-live Q4 2027. Thomas Kirmse (procurement) confirmed on 22 May that commercial and contract discussions follow the POC close. The one unmapped link is the internal approval / sign-off chain above procurement, which ties back to the unengaged economic buyer.

sources: Avoma POC closing 22 May 2026 (Rebecca Elias findings)
decision_criteria.narrative: The evaluation criteria are explicit and evidenced by Bosch's own structured POC scorecard delivered by Rebecca Elias on 22 May: end-to-end sourcing functionality and UX, high configurability, seamless sourcing-to-contracting integration, maturity of agentic AI (autonomous sourcing, quote comparison, third-party risk analysis), security and identity (BYOK / SCIM, API documentation and versioning), ESG and supplier-risk transparency, complex sourcing (auctions, non-tender allocation), and a clear data-migration strategy with live import/export. Bosch rated the platform a strong overall fit ('overall the fit is very good') while flagging out-of-the-box gaps that require customization. The RFP round 2 response must be shaped directly to this scorecard.

cro_panel.intro: One read per score, in language a CRO/CO can act on. We're well-positioned — the real risk is timing and sign-off, not a competitor.

blocks.how: A deal at 'Shortlisted' anchors near 55. Win signals and momentum lift the read to 63.
blocks.read: We're in it, with a slight edge.

Thomas Kirmse is the designated candidate and route to power yet is thinly present on calls, so champion strength is developing: deep operational advocacy exists, power-level advocacy does not.

commentary.deal_risk: Risk 21 is driven by Stage is ahead of the engagement/evidence the sweep found. Only observed negatives count, so these are real warning signs, not gaps.
commentary.win_position: Win 63 is carried by momentum 68 vs stage-expected 56; preference strength +0.40 (weight 20). Offsetting it: differentiation strength -0.40 (weight 20).
commentary.deal_momentum: Momentum 68 (moving forward): Next Step carries 17 dated milestone(s) — actively worked. Silence alone was not treated as backward motion.
commentary.evidence_coverage: Partial Read: 4 of 7 evidence dimensions seen (stage integrity, exec and champion, commercial, forward motion). It labels how much of the picture we have, never the primary scores.
commentary.customer_commitment: Commitment sits near the earned-from-zero floor: little observable customer investment surfaced. Commitment is earned, so a low value is honest, not a penalty.
commentary.forecast_confidence: Forecast confidence 46 is anchored on the stage win (63), nudged by commitment 8 and momentum 68, and dragged by risk 21. Read is Partial Read — a confidence flag (we know less), not a haircut on the score.

deal_movement.summary: Live and progressing. The POC closed positively on 22 May, the deal moved into commercial/contract discussions, and RFP round 2 documents arrived 29 Jun (response due 17 Jul). The buyer confirmed the POC phase concluded with no further alignment meetings, and a two-way email exchange on 2 Jun keeps the pulse live. The open technical/security items from the 26 May deep dive and the unengaged economic buyer are the drags on pace.

reasons.detail: Robert Bosch is a $1.2M Best-Case forecasted deal in an active 4-5 vendor tender with a July award imminent, yet there have been zero recorded calls or activities in the last 14 days and 31 days since any activity. Bosch sent RFP round 2 documents on Jun 29 (our response due 17.7) and the only movements in-window are Dirk's Next_Step field edits, not real engagement. If we stay dark through the down-select we risk losing the award by default in a field where the economic buyer has never once appeared on a call.
reasons.metric: 31 days since last activity; 0 calls/activities in last 14 days on $1.2M
reasons.ceo_ask: Ask Dirk why a $1.2M Bosch deal has had no live contact in 31 days with the July award looming, and whether the RFP round 2 response (due Jul 17) is on track.
reasons.summary: $1.2M Bosch deal silent 31 days as July award nears

critical_signals.text: Bosch is running a live multi-vendor tender and will close the proof of concept with all market participants before a July award, so the field is still open.
The 26 May integration deep dive left Bruno Streitenberger saying Bosch will reevaluate, after Lisa spent three weeks asking for API documentation and a Postman collection she never received live.
Bosch made BYOK and SCIM security architecture a mandate that Zycus does not yet offer and has verbally but not yet formally committed to build.
The budget owner has still never appeared on any call after seven months and a two-week proof of concept, and remains unengaged.
Thomas Kirmse confirmed on 22 May that Bosch is entering commercial and contract discussions, though pricing has not yet been exchanged.

confidence_signals.summary: Deal is live and engaged: a two-week on-site POC closed positively 22 May, RFP round 2 is in flight, and a two-way email exchange on 2 Jun keeps the pulse live. Momentum is real but the pace cannot meet the 31 July date.

north_star_verdict.math: Stage Shortlisted since 2026-04-30 (about 64 days in stage); close 2026-07-31 is 28 days out as of 2026-07-03. POC ran 11-22 May and closed positively. RFP round 2 documents received 29 Jun, response due 17 Jul. Remaining gates after RFP2: implementation-partner selection (in progress, EY ruled out), commercial/pricing (just starting, promised for June), economic-buyer approval (buyer never engaged), MSA/SOW, and InfoSec blocked on BYOK/SCIM written commitment. On the buyer's own timeline awarding was targeted for July, project start August 2026, go-live Q4 2027, so the 31 July close realistically slips 60-90 days into Q4 2026.
evidence: Two-week on-site POC ran 11-22 May 2026 across S2C, P2O and technical tracks (POC kickoff call, 11 May 2026).

Closing call 22 May 2026: Rebecca Elias delivered detailed positive findings ('your platform provides a very solid foundation'); Thomas Kirmse stated Bosch is 'in the contract discussions and commercial discussion' and will 'step into further discussions in June'.
Technical deep dive 26 May 2026: Bruno Streitenberger said 'based on this meeting, we will reevaluate our evaluation' after Lisa's API-documentation questions went unmet for three weeks.
Next Step (Salesforce): RFP round 2 documents received 29 Jun 2026, response due 17 Jul; implementation partners being contacted in parallel; call Thomas Kirmse 25 Jun.
Buyer email received 2026-06-02 confirms two-way engagement within the last month (deal pulse: live).
north_star_verdict.headline: Genuinely healthy, deeply engaged deal: a two-week on-site POC closed 22 May with strong feedback and Bosch is entering commercials, but the 31 July close cannot survive RFP round 2 (due 17 Jul), partner selection, pricing, economic-buyer approval and contracting.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Best Case is not defensible for a 31 July close; move the close date to Q4 2026 (Oct/Nov) since RFP round 2 alone runs to 17 Jul before pricing, EB approval and contracting can begin.

flags: Close the technical trust gap: deliver the live API/Postman demo, Swagger docs and data import/export session Lisa has requested since early May, since Bruno said the 26 May session would trigger a reevaluation.

Convert operational advocacy into a power champion: Nordmann and Elias give deep access but none sells to the budget owner, so recruit Thomas Kirmse or an exec sponsor to carry Zycus into the July award.
Get ahead of the July award: map the RFP round 2 response to the relevant stakeholder scorecard and co-author a mutual close plan resequenced to a realistic Q4 2026 date.
best_practice_check.summary: Three win-strategy levers matter most before the July award: close the technical trust gap that triggered Bruno's reevaluation, convert operational advocates into a power champion who reaches the budget owner, and shape the RFP round 2 response to the buyer scorecard while resequencing the close plan to a credible Q4 date.

customer_preference.evidence: Positive POC verdict ('very solid foundation', 'overall the fit is very good') and deep Zycus-specific engagement, but many out-of-the-box gaps flagged and Bruno said Bosch 'will reevaluate' with other vendors still live.

competitive_position.summary: Bosch is running a live 4-5 vendor tender with a July award imminent and will 'close the PoC with all market participants' before deciding, so the strongest current threat is the undisclosed finalist field in the active down-select, not any status-quo option. No rival is named on any call or in Salesforce, which remains a genuine knowledge gap and the priority to close with Thomas Kirmse. The clearest characterised alternative is the status quo of fragmented best-of-breed tools the platform would replace. Zycus's differentiators are organically-built agentic AI (Merlin) and proven live SAP S/4HANA integration, both praised in the POC.

competitors.how_we_win: Position the unified 12-product S2P suite as single-platform simplification versus tool sprawl, and de-risk the customization load Bosch flagged with a clear implementation and partner plan so status-quo comfort does not win by default.

The agentic story landed as promise, not proof, so the near-term lever is to demonstrate mature autonomous sourcing and quote-comparison agents against Bosch's own use cases.
